items.done: Pick a model in the TUI → it's still the model next launch.

items.title: @-file mentions

items.title: Calmer TUI (less firehose)
items.summary: tui/tool-display.ts (pure, 14 tests): toolDisplay(name,args)→{icon,verb,detail} computed at dispatch time — clean verbs + a per-tool verb map (read/wrote/ran/searched/saw screen/git·sub/…), abbrevPath (temp dirs incl NSIRD → basename, $HOME→~, deep→…/last/two), never raw JSON. Detail comes from ARGS not raw output (kills the temp-path leak; output→model only). On error a red firstLine tail stays visible. partitionBlocks groups consecutive tool rows into one indented cluster. transcript.tsx renders the parts purely; app.tsx reducer carries icon/verb/detail + errorLine. Design note: docs/tui-smooth-design.md. DEFERRED (needs Jason's interactive design): collapse/expand folding + the 'does it feel calm' judgment — Hashmark turned out to be a web app, not a transferable TUI reference.
items.done: A multi-tool turn reads as a calm grouped feed — no raw temp paths, no JSON dumps, indented clusters. Interactive collapse/expand flagged for brainstorming.

items.title: Dark factory (self-improving codebase)
items.summary: factory/ module: triage→plan→execute→verify→commit. argo improve + argo factory approve.
items.done: Live end-to-end: verifier correctly rejected bad model output.

items.title: Autonomy ladder (L1–L4, kernel-bounded)
items.summary: factory/run.ts now runs at a configurable autonomy level: L1 suggest (plan, no branch) · L2 implement (branch+execute+verify, stop for review) · L3 commit (no push) · L4 push. resolveAutonomyLevel(sub,env): improve=L1, approve=ARGO_AUTONOMY_LEVEL (default 4). Split commitAndPush→commitSlice+pushBranch; new CycleResult statuses implemented + committed{pushed}. Kernel is_protected_path still blocks skeleton/brainstem at every level. L5 auto-merge + low-risk classifier deferred (riskiest rung).
items.done: argo factory approve with ARGO_AUTONOMY_LEVEL=2|3|4 stops exactly at implement/commit/push; 15 run.test.ts incl. resolveAutonomyLevel.

items.title: Autonomy L5 (auto-merge low-risk)
items.summary: factory/merge.ts: assessMergeRisk fails-closed through 3 gates — armed (ARGO_AUTONOMY_ALLOW_MERGE, default OFF), safe target (resolveMergeTarget default factory/integration, never main/master, --no-ff never force), low-risk (limbs/reflexes/memory only via O11, no dep/env/config/migration file, ≤20 files & ≤400 diff lines). MAX_AUTONOMY_LEVEL 4→5. run.ts: after L4 push, an armed+low-risk slice merges into the integration branch (mergeIntoTarget restores HEAD, aborts on conflict/missing target → stays L4). The git lifecycle runs outside the kernel assess() gate, so the classifier is the whole safety story; it's pure + 11 merge.test.ts. Merge git sequence smoke-verified (clean + conflict-abort + missing-target).
items.done: A low-risk verified slice auto-merges when armed; anything failing the classifier stops at L4 push. Default OFF (riskiest rung — opt-in).

items.title: Compartmentalized self-repair (body model)
items.summary: factory/compartments.ts: classifyCompartment(file)→tier (skeleton/brainstem/limbs/reflexes/memory), compartmentMaxAutonomy(tier)→max L (skeleton 0 · brainstem 2 · limbs/reflexes/memory 5), autonomyCapForFiles(files)→most-restrictive cap. run.ts clamps the requested autonomy level to the cap of the files a slice ACTUALLY touched (mixed slice → minimum). A brainstem fix can't auto-commit even at L4; only limbs/reflexes/memory reach the full ladder. is_protected_path stays the hard boundary; this is the soft policy it can't express.
items.done: A tier map exists + the factory refuses to exceed a compartment's max autonomy: limb fixes flow autonomously, brainstem/skeleton don't. 12 compartments.test.ts.

items.title: MCP client
items.summary: Dependency-free stdio JSON-RPC client + mount.ts. Mounted in prepareRun.
items.done: Protocol unit-tested; live needs a real server + config fix (MCP-1).

items.title: Writable zones beyond the repo
items.summary: write_file now writes outside the repo into bounded, approval-gated writable zones (default ~/Desktop+~/Downloads, ARGO_WRITABLE_DIRS override) instead of hard-refusing. TS-only: the kernel already returned Ask for out-of-root paths; the tool was stricter than the boundary. ~-expansion + prefix-collision-safe zone check. Out-of-zone still refused (backstop vs yes-fatigue).
items.done: Live-verified: write_file wrote directly to ~/Desktop in one step — no repo pollution, no shell copy.

items.title: Readable zones (read across the workspace)
items.summary: read_file hard-refused out-of-repo reads, so Argo couldn't read sibling-repo skills (e.g. ~/Documents/GitHub/theft-kit). Mirror of SCOPE-1: read_file now reads from readable zones — default = the project's PARENT dir (sibling repos in the same workspace) + the writable zones; ARGO_READABLE_DIRS override. Shared isInZone/expandHome helper. Follow-up: secret-filename read-guard (.env/keys) even in-zone.

items.title: Secret-hygiene hardening
items.summary: gitleaks pre-commit hook + .example twins, installed by install.sh.
items.done: A secret-shaped string can't be committed (verified: hook runs gitleaks protect --staged).

items.title: One-line curl install
items.summary: bootstrap.sh clones Argo into ~/argo (ARGO_DIR override, default branch — no pin) then runs install.sh. install.sh has no interactive prompts so piped installs can't crash (no /dev/tty probe needed). Documented in README.
items.done: Clone+install logic verified via local clone smoke; curl-pipe one-liner activates when the repo flips public.

items.title: Use any MCP (consume)
items.summary: readMcpConfig accepts mcpServers key + discovers ./.mcp.json (project) over ~/.argo/mcp.json (user). argo mcp list.
items.done: Config discovery shipped; live consume needs a real server.

items.title: Make + hook in at runtime
items.summary: mount_mcp tool (live runtime mount, kernel-gated) + build-mcp-server skill (scaffold on demand).
items.done: mount_mcp registers a server's tools into the live registry; skill teaches scaffolding.

items.title: Be a server (serve)
items.summary: argo mcp serve — exposes Argo tools over MCP stdio, kernel-gated + read-only allowlist. console.log→stderr keeps the protocol stream clean.
items.done: Live-verified: real McpClient drove argo mcp serve subprocess — handshake, tools/list, allow→execute, out-of-scope refused, non-allowlisted refused. Claude Code integration untested.
